X-Men: Days of Future Past (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $233,921,534Details
International Box Office $513,941,241
Worldwide Box Office $747,862,775
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $25,359,245
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $52,558,588
Total $77,917,833
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The X-Men ensemble fights a war for the survival of the species across two time periods. The characters from the original “X-Men” film trilogy join forces with their younger selves from the past, in order to change a major historical event and fight in an epic battle that could save our future.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$90,823,660 (38.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.58 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:31.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$200,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,996 opening theaters/4,001 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $342,435,933

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 23rd, 2014 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: May 21st, 2014 (Wide) (Australia)
May 21st, 2014 (Wide) (Belgium)
May 21st, 2014 (Wide) (Taiwan)
May 21st, 2014 (Wide) (Iceland)
May 21st, 2014 (Wide) (Philippines)
Video Release: October 14th, 2014 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of intense sci-fi violence and action, some suggestive material, nudity and language.
(Rating bulletin 2320, 4/30/2014)
Running Time: 131 minutes
Franchise: X-Men
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Gratuitous Cameos, Same Role, Multiple Actors, Scene in End Credits, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D
Geographical setting: Moscow, China, Paris, Vietnam
Plot point: Assassination, Government Corruption, Mutants, Political, Prison Break, Rescue, Robot, Superhuman, Time Travel
Social setting: Dystopia, Vietnam War
Source material: Marvel Comics
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 1970s, 2020s
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Super Hero
Production/Financing Companies: Bad Hat Harry Productions, Donners’ Company, Simon Kinberg, Ingenious Media, Marvel Studios, 20th Century Fox, Genre Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

The X-Men ensemble fights a war for the survival of the species across two time periods. The characters from the original “X-Men” film trilogy join forces with their younger selves from the past, in order to change a major historical event and fight in an epic battle that could save our future.

Opening Weekend:$90,823,660 (38.8% of total gross)

Domestic Share:31.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)

Theater counts:3,996 opening theaters/4,001 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $342,435,933
